description: LPETG — the canonical sortase A (SrtA) recognition motif (a single instance of the Gram-positive LPXTG cell-wall sorting signal); SrtA cleaves between the Thr and Gly (LPET|G), forming an acyl-enzyme thioester that is resolved by transpeptidation onto an N-terminal oligoglycine acceptor, making LPETG the standard C-terminal handle for site-specific protein ligation (sortagging) and conjugation.

references.authors: Mazmanian SK, Liu G, Ton-That H, Schneewind O
references.title: Staphylococcus aureus sortase, an enzyme that anchors surface proteins to the cell wall

references.title: Purification and characterization of sortase, the transpeptidase that cleaves surface proteins of Staphylococcus aureus at the LPXTG motif

references.title: Site-specific C-terminal and internal loop labeling of proteins using sortase-mediated reactions

label: LPETG_tag
note: Sortase A (SrtA) recognition motif and a single instance of the Gram-positive LPXTG cell-wall sorting signal (Leu-Pro-Glu-Thr-Gly). SrtA cleaves the scissile peptide bond between the P1 Thr and the P1' Gly (LPET|G), forming a covalent acyl-enzyme thioester intermediate at the active-site Cys; this intermediate is resolved by nucleophilic attack of an N-terminal oligoglycine (Gly-n, typically GGG/GGGGG) acceptor, ligating the upstream protein to the acceptor and regenerating an LPETGG... junction. Whole-part feature spanning all 5 residues; this is a single atomic functional element with no functional sub-features. The X position of the LPXTG consensus is most commonly Glu (E) in efficient engineering substrates. Source-verified to UniProt P02976 (Protein A) Motif 'LPXTG sorting signal' at positions 482-486, 5/5 identity.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt P02976 (SPA_STAA8, Immunoglobulin G-binding protein A, S. aureus NCTC 8325, Swiss-Prot PE=1 reviewed), positions 482-486 (1-based), explicit Motif annotation 'LPXTG sorting signal'; sequence LPETG independently fetched and confirmed exact 5/5 match

functional_claims.label: LPETG is an instance of the LPXTG sortase recognition motif; sortase A cleaves between the Thr and the Gly (LPET|G)

value.scissile_bond: between P1 Thr and P1' Gly (LPET|G)
value.x_position_note: Glu (E) is commonly found at the X position of efficient natural and engineering SrtA substrates

source.quote: Purification and characterization of sortase, the transpeptidase that cleaves surface proteins of Staphylococcus aureus at the LPXTG motif

functional_claims.label: Sortase A is a transpeptidase: it cleaves LPXTG to form an acyl-enzyme thioester at the active-site Cys, then transfers the acyl group to an N-terminal oligoglycine acceptor (transpeptidation)

value.step_1: cleavage of the Thr-Gly bond of LPXTG and formation of a covalent acyl-enzyme thioester intermediate at the active-site cysteine
value.step_2: resolution of the thioester by nucleophilic attack of an N-terminal oligoglycine (Gly-n) amine, ligating the LPET-acyl group to the acceptor
value.net_result: site-specific ligation of an LPXTG-tagged protein to a Gly-n-bearing partner, reconstituting an LPETGG... junction
value.natural_function: anchoring surface proteins to the Gram-positive cell-wall peptidoglycan (via the lipid II / pentaglycine cross-bridge as natural acceptor)

source.quote: Staphylococcus aureus sortase, an enzyme that anchors surface proteins to the cell wall

functional_claims.label: LPETG is the native LPXTG sorting signal of S. aureus Protein A (SpA); native SpA carries the exact LPETG sequence in its precursor

value.origin_protein: Immunoglobulin G-binding protein A (Protein A / SpA), Staphylococcus aureus (UniProt P02976)
value.also_present_in: FnbA (UniProt P14738) at positions 982-986, and many other Gram-positive surface proteins as the conserved LPXTG class signal
value.biological_context: C-terminal cell-wall sorting signal directing SrtA-mediated covalent anchoring to peptidoglycan

source.quote: Protein A is released into the Staphylococcus aureus culture supernatant with an unprocessed sorting signal

functional_claims.label: LPETG is the standard C-terminal handle for sortase-mediated protein ligation (sortagging): a protein carrying C-terminal LPETG can be site-specifically conjugated to molecules bearing an N-terminal glycine

value.handle: C-terminal LPETG on the protein of interest
value.acceptor: N-terminal (oligo)glycine on the partner (peptide, protein, small molecule, surface, D-peptide, or branched peptide)
uses: C-terminal and internal-loop labeling of proteins
value.founding_in_vitro_demonstration: use of LPETG for in vitro protein engineering / conjugation to (D)-peptides, branched peptides, and small molecules bearing an N-terminal glycine

source.quote: Sortase-mediated protein ligation: a new method for protein engineering

functional_claims.label: LPETG must be placed at (or near) the C-terminus of the protein of interest and in an accessible, unstructured region; the Gly that follows it is consumed/relocated by the ligation

value.position: C-terminal of the protein of interest (commonly POI-LPETG-His6 so an affinity tag downstream is removed upon ligation)
value.structural_requirement: the LPXTG motif should lie in a flexible, solvent-accessible / unstructured region for efficient cleavage
value.junction_after_ligation: LPETGG... (the acceptor's N-terminal Gly residues become part of the product)

source.quote: Site-specific C-terminal and internal loop labeling of proteins using sortase-mediated reactions

functional_claims.label: Host-agnostic sequence element: LPETG is a short peptide motif and the sortase enzyme is supplied separately, so the handle functions independently of the expression host

value.element_type: sortase recognition / cell-wall sorting signal (peptide motif)
value.host_dependence: none for the motif itself; ligation requires sortase A supplied in vitro (or co-expressed in vivo)
value.enzyme_origin: Staphylococcus aureus sortase A (SrtA); commonly the evolved pentamutant eSrtA for enhanced activity

source.quote: Sortase-mediated protein ligation: a new method for protein engineering

functional_claims.label: The catalyst, not the motif, is the main engineered variant: the evolved SrtA pentamutant (eSrtA) has greatly improved activity toward LPETG; orthogonal sortases instead recognize altered motifs (LAXTG / LPXSG) rather than LPETG

value.motif_variants: LPXTG class; X is most often Glu in efficient substrates (LPETG); other X residues occur in natural signals
value.evolved_catalyst: eSrtA pentamutant (P94R/D160N/D165A/K190E/K196T), reported with markedly improved activity toward LPETG
value.reported_improvement: ~140-fold increased activity vs wild-type SrtA (quantitative value from a primary text not directly accessed)
value.orthogonal_motifs: engineered sortases reprogrammed to recognize LAXTG and LPXSG, independent of canonical LPETG, enabling dual labeling

source.quote: A general strategy for the evolution of bond-forming enzymes using yeast display
